what: Real split-K=8 control on the fused-hint baseline with default 16x64 gluon reduction
why: The recent fixed-input bests came from split-K reduction retunes, but the branch never cleanly measured the true 8-way split geometry against the current fused-hint baseline. This isolates the main GEMM split shape change while restoring the library default reduction tile so the effect of real split-8 can be measured directly.

what: Keep real split-K=8 and retile only the gluon reduction to 8x32
why: The current branch has not measured the strongest recent reduction retile together with the true 8-way split geometry. This keeps all non-split shapes fixed, preserves the real split-8 main kernel, and changes only the reduction tile to test whether the extra partial restores the benefit of a denser reduction grid.

what: Keep real split-K=8 and change only the gluon reduction row tile to 8x64
why: The best recent fixed-input result came from a denser split-K reduction grid, but 8x32 may be over-fragmenting the N dimension. This variant keeps the same real split-8 main kernel and default N tile while halving only the M tile of the reduction stage.

what: Keep real split-K=8 and retile the gluon reduction to 4x32
why: The split-K branch already showed sensitivity to reduction geometry, and increasing the split count from 7 to 8 raises the chance that a more parallel reduction wins. This variant keeps the main kernel fixed and tests the most aggressive still-legal nearby reduce tile.

what: Restore the strongest valid split-K=7 plus 8x32 reduction as a leaderboard confirmation candidate
why: The real split-8 line is invalid because BLOCK_SIZE_K=128 fails Triton compilation in the preshuffle kernel. The best remaining evidence is the split-K=7 plus 8x32 reduction branch, which is the strongest benchmarked valid candidate and the right one to confirm on leaderboard.
override: This overlaps with v280 semantically, but the split-8 detour proved invalid and the best valid branch should be re-established explicitly on the current session before spending leaderboard quota.

what: Keep the repaired split-K=7 path and widen only the gluon reduction to 8x64
why: The strongest valid branch uses split-K=7 with an 8x32 reduction retile. This variant stays on the same repaired main kernel and tests whether the smaller row tile remains beneficial once the reduction width returns to the library default.

what: Keep the repaired split-K=7 path and narrow only the gluon reduction width to 16x32
why: The current valid search has 16x64 as the library default and 8x32 as the strongest benchmarked retile, but that leaves row-tile and width-tile effects confounded. This control keeps the default row tile and changes only the reduction width so the split-K gain can be attributed more cleanly when quota reopens.

what: Keep the repaired split-K=7 path and retile the gluon reduction to 4x32
why: With test quota closed, the best use of time is to stage the remaining nearby valid reduction control. This keeps the known-good split-K=7 main kernel and probes the most parallel legal reduce tile so the next submission can resume the search immediately when quota reopens.

what: Promote the valid split-K=7 8x64 reduction branch as the next safer candidate after the 8x32 leaderboard revert
why: v292 showed that the 8x32 reduction branch is not leaderboard-stable even though it passes the lighter test gate, and the leaderboard artifacts were unavailable. The 8x64 neighbor already passed server test, stays closer to the library default, and is the safer next branch to benchmark and potentially confirm.
